{{Quote|Talos Merkin is a veteran of countless campaigns. He's one of our best pilots. We can't afford to lose him. Fire hits his wing. The ship is struggling. Its path is unsteady. It's spiraling out of control. No! Not Talos!|Princess Leia Organa, during the Battle of Yavin|<ref name="Journal" />}}
'''Talos Merkin''' was a veteran [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] [[Rebel pilot/Legends|pilot]] of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. He participated in numerous campaigns prior to joining the Rebellion's assault on the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire's]] [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|Death Star]] [[Battle station/Legends|battle station]] during the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]]. Although [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]] regarded him as one of the Rebels' best [[Pilot/Legends|pilots]], Merkin was [[Death/Legends|killed]] when his fighter was shot down near the end of the battle, which nevertheless culminated in the [[Death Star/Legends|Death Star's]] destruction.<ref name="Journal" />

==Behind the scenes==
{{Quote|[…] Red 12 finally gets a name, chosen after sorting through a number of candidates. They were Naeco (original ''X-wing'' game), Captain Ernek Marskan (same), Fin Danglot (''Galaxy Guide 1''), Travis (a blonde woman from Marvel's retelling of ''A New Hope'' back in the ''Droids'' kid's comic – how's ''that'' for obscure?), and Talos Merkin (''Captive to Evil'')|Jason Fry, ''The Essential Guide to Warfare'' endnotes|<ref name="Warfare">{{Tumblr|subdomain=jasonfry|url=post/24614452839/eg-to-warfare-endnotes-pt-10|name=Jason Fry's Dorkery|text=EG to Warfare: Endnotes Pt. 10|quote=[…] [[Red Twelve/Legends|Red 12]] finally gets a name, chosen after sorting through a number of candidates. They were [[Puck Naeco/Legends|Naeco]] (original [[Star Wars: X-Wing (video game)|''X-wing'']] game), Captain [[Ernek Marskan]] (same), [[Fin Danglot]] ([[Galaxy Guide 1: A New Hope|''Galaxy Guide 1'']]), [[Milar Travis|Travis]] (a blonde woman from Marvel's retelling of [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''A New Hope'']] back in the [[Droids (1986) 8|''Droids'']] kid's comic – how's ''that'' for obscure?), and Talos Merkin ([[Star Wars Journal: Captive to Evil|''Captive to Evil'']]).}}</ref>}}
Talos Merkin was created for the [[1998]] young-readers story ''[[Star Wars Journal: Captive to Evil]]'', written by [[Jude Watson]]. The story, which is told through Princess Leia Organa's personal journal entries, identifies Merkin as flying under the [[Callsign/Legends|call sign]] [[Gold Five/Legends|Gold Five]] during the Battle of Yavin.<ref name="Journal" /> However, the [[1995]] ''[[Premiere Limited]]'' expansion set of the [[Star Wars Customizable Card Game|''Star Wars'' Customizable Card Game]] had already identified Rebel pilot [[Davish Krail/Legends|Davish Krail]] as Gold Five during the battle,<ref name="SWCCG">{{CCG|set=Premiere Limited|cardname=Pops}}</ref> which was later reaffirmed by the [[2008]] ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''.<ref name="CSWE">{{CSWECite|I|351|Gold Five}}</ref>

For the [[2012]] reference book ''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'', author [[Jason Fry]] considered Talos Merkin for the previously unnamed role of [[Red Twelve/Legends|Red Twelve]] during the Battle of Yavin. From a group of five candidates, Fry ultimately settled on [[Puck Naeco/Legends|Puck Naeco]],<ref name="Warfare" /> a pilot from the [[1993]] [[Wikipedia:Personal computer|PC]] [[Star Wars video games|game]] [[Star Wars: X-Wing (video game)|''Star Wars: X-Wing'']].<ref name="X-Wing">[[Star Wars: X-Wing (video game)|''Star Wars: X-Wing'']]</ref>
